pattern: Treat the numbers as nodes of a ten-way prefix tree and emit them in pre-order: multiply by ten to descend, increment to move sideways, and climb when a branch overflows n.
hints: Sorting the numbers as strings works but costs more than the required linear time; think of the digits as a tree you traverse in order.
From a current value, the next lexicographic number is that value times ten if it stays within n; otherwise advance to the next sibling.
When times-ten overflows n (or ends in nine), strip trailing nines by dividing out, then add one — a pre-order walk of the 10-ary prefix tree producing all n numbers in order.

edge_inputs.description: exactly nine then ten reorders heavily
boundary right after a power of ten

stress.description: random n within range exercising irregular stopping points
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [rng.randint(1, max(1, size))]
stress.note: validates the iterative prefix-tree walk against the string-sort oracle
